description: Seshat Self-Update System — Add seshat update and seshat update --check commands so users can discover and install new versions without remembering which package manager they used. Queries GitHub Releases API as single source of truth, verifies downloads with SHA256 checksums, and prints background update notices on CLI commands (cached for 24h).

userStories.title: Version cache system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a file-based version cache so the CLI doesn't hit the GitHub API on every invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: Cache file at dirs::data_dir()/seshat/version-check.json
Schema: { latest_version: string, checked_at: ISO8601 }
Cache considered stale after 24 hours
Missing, empty, or corrupted cache treated as stale → triggers live check
On successful live check → writes cache immediately
On failed live check → does NOT write cache (retains old stale entry)
Creates seshat data directory if it doesn't exist
Unit tests: fresh/stale/missing/corrupted cache behavior, 24h TTL boundary
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: seshat update --check command
userStories.description: As a seshat user, I want to check whether a newer version exists without installing anything.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Update { check: bool } variant to args.rs CLI enum
seshat update --check uses 24h cache; no network if cache fresh
Prints 'Seshat vX.Y.Z is available (current: vA.B.C). Run seshat update to upgrade.' or 'Seshat is up to date (vA.B.C).'
Homebrew install: prints 'Seshat vX.Y.Z is available. You installed via Homebrew. Run brew upgrade seshat.'
Queries GitHub Releases API /repos/KSDaemon/seshat/releases/latest, parses tag_name, strips v prefix, compares with semver
No binary asset for current target triple → treats as no update (no message, up to date)
Network errors: prints 'Could not check for updates: [reason]' to stderr, exit 1
Unit tests: fresh cache no network, stale triggers call, up-to-date message, version available message, brew message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Self-update — detection, download and verification
userStories.description: As a seshat user, I want to download and verify the latest binary securely before it replaces my current installation.
acceptanceCriteria: detect_install_method(): path contains /Cellar/ or symlink to /Cellar/ → Homebrew; else → Direct
If Homebrew detected: prints brew upgrade message, exits 1
Finds binary asset matching current target triple (aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu) in GitHub release assets
Downloads sha256sums.txt from same release, parses SHA256 line for matched archive
Downloads binary archive to temp directory with progress indicator, computes SHA256, verifies against checksums file
SHA256 mismatch → abort with error, delete temp files, existing binary untouched
Extracts tar.gz archive (expects seshat-{target}-v{version}/seshat inside)
Corrupted archive or missing binary → error, existing binary untouched
All network calls have 15s timeout
Windows: prints 'Self-update not supported on Windows. Use cargo install seshat or download from GitHub Releases.' exits 1
Unit tests: install method detection (Cellar, symlink, direct, fallback), platform target mapping, SHA256 pass/fail, tar.gz extraction pass/fail
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Self-update — gatekeeper check and atomic replace
userStories.description: As a seshat user, I want the update to safely replace my current binary without corruption.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-flight check: spawns extracted binary with --version. Success → proceed. Killed: 9 (macOS Gatekeeper) → print xattr -d com.apple.quarantine instructions, clean up, exit 1
Resolves symlinks before replacing (replace actual binary, not symlink)
Atomic replace via fs::rename(temp → current_exe)
If rename fails with EXDEV (cross-filesystem) → fall back to copy + remove
Permission denied on exe path → print 'Permission denied. Try: sudo seshat update'
If installed via cargo install (checked via ~/.cargo/.crates2.json or ~/.cargo/.crates.toml) → print cargo note after update
Cargo detection files missing/unparseable → skip cargo note gracefully
Prints 'Seshat updated to vX.Y.Z.' and exits 0
Never touches existing binary until all verification passes
Unit tests: gatekeeper kill simulation, cargo detection (both file formats, missing, corrupted), replace with symlink resolution
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Background update notice on CLI commands
userStories.description: As a seshat user, I want to see a gentle notice when a newer version exists, so I know to run seshat update without actively checking.
acceptanceCriteria: At startup of ANY command (except seshat update and seshat update --check), prints update notice to stderr if newer version binaries available
Uses 24h version cache — max one GitHub API call per day
Notice format: 'Seshat vX.Y.Z is available (current: vA.B.C). Run seshat update to upgrade.'
Printed for: serve, scan, status, review, init, uninstall, --help, --version
NOT printed during seshat update or seshat update --check
Network failures silently skip (no error, no delay, no output)
No binary asset found for current target → no notice (silent)
check_and_print_update_notice() called once in run() before command dispatch, suppressed only for Command::Update variants
All output goes to stderr — invisible to MCP protocol consumers
Integration tests: notice printed for scan/status/serve, suppressed for update/update --check, network failure silent skip
Typecheck passes
